title: #support: 3319998888
text:
Ben Carter: Hey team, getting 401 invalid_api_key intermittently after users copy the key from the dashboard. Happens only for keys pasted into our mobile app. Example response: 401 {"error":"invalid_api_key"} 
Lina (support): Sorry to hear that — can you paste the exact curl you used and the raw header value? 
Ben Carter: here’s the repro: 
```
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer <PASTED_KEY>" https://api.redwood.ai/v1/engines/text/generate
```
Ben Carter: I ran a hexdump of the pasted value (dashboard copy -> paste into terminal -> hexdump): 
```
00000000 41 42 43 ad 44 45 46 47 48 49 4a 4b 4c 4d 4e 4f |ABC.AD...HIJKLMNO|
00000010 50 51 52 |PQR|
```
Ben Carter: you can see 0xAD in the middle (soft hyphen). users copy the key and the app rejects it. 
Lina (support): oh interesting, we normally see zwsp/zwj but not soft hyphen. can you try removing it locally and retry? quick command: 
...

title: #support: 1982345678

Ben Carter: do they have request IDs / traces?

Aisha Patel: They provided two request traces: tr-5a9b2d10 (fir...
text:
Aisha Patel: heads-up: BrightHire reports a streaming completion cutting off mid-token and then seeing duplicate billing for the same user request. Affects JS + Python SDKs. Started 2026-03-12 ~14:03 UTC.

Ben Carter: do they have request IDs / traces?

Aisha Patel: They provided two request traces: tr-5a9b2d10 (first) and tr-5a9b2d11 (retry). Client saw 'data: [DONE]' then connection closed, then retried and billed twice (~120 tokens each). They uploaded a pcap: s3://redwood-triage/brighthire/pcap_march12.pcap

Kai Zhang: looked at pcap + edge logs. Summary: edge shows a connection close from client side after ~62s idle while runtime was still emitting chunks. Server continued to push, but TCP was closed. Client opened a new stream with a new auth token. Likely causes: corporate proxy idle reaping (60s common default) or HTTP/2 connection TTL reuse bug.

Kai Zhang: edge log excerpt f...

title: #support: 1842001234
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — CI deploy to staging failing with 401s for the last ~20 minutes. All PR merges that trigger the pipeline are failing at the "fetch artifacts" step which calls /v1/internal/artifacts with service account token. Error log:

Ravi Patel: can you paste logs? Which SA and token type? Is this the new s...
text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — CI deploy to staging failing with 401s for the last ~20 minutes. All PR merges that trigger the pipeline are failing at the "fetch artifacts" step which calls /v1/internal/artifacts with service account token. Error log:

Ravi Patel: can you paste logs? Which SA and token type? Is this the new short-lived token rollout?

Ava Chen: This is using ci-deploy-sa (k8s serviceaccount) via our OIDC token-exchange. The pipeline logs show:
```
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $CI_TOKEN" https://api.redwood.ai/internal/artifacts
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
{"error":"token_expired","message":"token is expired or invalid"}
```
We rotated keys last week but only for a different SA. This SA was supposed to be using ephemeral tokens with 10m TTL. The pipeline expects 15m. Repro: run job locally with kc exec and keep job idle for 12m, then call same endpoint -> 401.

Priya Singh: are ...

title: #support: 1823456789
preview:
Ava Chen: Customer on Standard plan complaining about repeated 429s during a 3-min surge, they say traffic shifted from us-east to eu-west and then errors started. They've attached a curl and response.
Diego Morales: Can you paste the response body? 429 has a few shapes (global rate limit vs regional). Also: were th...
text:
Ava Chen: Customer on Standard plan complaining about repeated 429s during a 3-min surge, they say traffic shifted from us-east to eu-west and then errors started. They've attached a curl and response.
Diego Morales: Can you paste the response body? 429 has a few shapes (global rate limit vs regional). Also: were they hitting embeddings or generation API?
Ava Chen: Generation. Here's the response snippet they sent:
```
HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests
Content-Type: application/json
{
 \"error\": {\"message\": \"Rate limit exceeded\", \"type\": \"quota\", \"region\": \"eu-west-1\"}
}
```
Priya Nair: Region field is interesting. If failover routed them to eu-west because of us-east saturation, but their account has lower reserved capacity in eu-west then Surface will trigger per-region throttles. Does the customer have a dedicated / reserved capacity contract? 
Ava Chen: No, they're on St...

text:
Jordan Blake: escalating on behalf of Acme Payments. They report a spike in 5xx/403 for EU users since ~03:15Z and claim requests are being routed to us-east where token validation fails. sample request id=01H8KQ8R

Kai Chen: can you paste the exact curl the customer used and any response headers or trace id?

Jordan Blake: copied from customer:

```
curl -v -H "Host: api.acme.com" \
 -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" \
 https://api.redwood.example/v1/payments/charge
```

response headers (customer):
```
HTTP/2 403
x-region: us-east
x-envoy-upstream-service-time: 312
server: envoy
```

Jordan Blake: they insist traffic should land in eu-west. token issuer is eu-kms and when routed to us-east auth fails.

Anya Patel: did we touch any host/route rewrite logic recently? I remember a change for cookie sticky sessions last week.

title: #support: 1772951300

text:
Naomi Feldman: Seeing Acme Analytics report streams dropping mid-response (SSE). Client says "connection closed" / EOF. Anyone else seeing this?

Aisha Rahman: I saw a small spike in downstream disconnects in us-east after todays edge deploy. Digging.

Ethan Park: Can you grab request ids + approx timestamps? Also which endpoint (OpenAI-compat vs native)?

Naomi Feldman: OpenAI-compat chat.completions, streaming=true. Timestamps ~22:10-22:40 UTC. Ill attach customer logs to SUP-4821.

Connor O'Brien: +1 I had a similar complaint last week (different customer) but they never escalated. Could be same thing.

Aisha Rahman: If its edge timeout/buffering, we should check streaming route config. Ill loop in #incidents if it keeps climbing.

Ethan Park: Ill open a PR to bump SSE idle timeout + disable buffering for that route.

title: #support: 1772010331
preview:
Dev Patel: Quick q on the SLA table wording before we lock it. For Enterprise P0/P1 update cadence — do we say “every 30 min” or “hourly”? Also: how do we handle Jira status “Waiting on Customer” without it looking like we’re missing update SLAs?

Rachel Kim: My instinct is hourly as the default. 30 min reads like a...
text:
Dev Patel: Quick q on the SLA table wording before we lock it. For Enterprise P0/P1 update cadence — do we say “every 30 min” or “hourly”? Also: how do we handle Jira status “Waiting on Customer” without it looking like we’re missing update SLAs?

Rachel Kim: My instinct is hourly as the default. 30 min reads like a hard promise and we’ll get burned when we’re juggling multiple bridges.

Owen Phillips: +1. For true incident (Sev0-ish) we often update every ~30 min anyway, but it’s not consistent enough to write as SLA. Also depends if we’re in #incidents with incident bot posting.

Priya Shah: We should separate “internal updates in bridge” vs “customer-facing email cadence”. Customers care about the email cadence.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE side: we can support “hourly” reliably for customer-facing updates during active investigation. 30 min is doable during a major incident, but not ...

title: #support: 1770190088
preview: Dina Alvarez: Heads up — Acme AI is asking if we can align the *daily usage export* to their timezone (they’re on America/Los_Angeles). They’re seeing “spikes” at day boundaries b/c our export rolls over at 00:00 UTC. Any supported way to do this today? (ticket from their side references “daily usage export tz fix”)...
text:
Dina Alvarez: Heads up — Acme AI is asking if we can align the *daily usage export* to their timezone (they’re on America/Los_Angeles). They’re seeing “spikes” at day boundaries b/c our export rolls over at 00:00 UTC. Any supported way to do this today? (ticket from their side references “daily usage export tz fix”).

Supportbot: :ticket: Linked case: SUP-28522 (Usage export timezone misalignment)

Mei Lin: Today: daily export “day” is hard-coded UTC. It’s literally `date = event_ts::date` after normalizing to UTC in the pipeline. No tz parameter.

Cole Summers: +1. Usage API & export tables assume UTC day boundaries. Changing it globally would be a breaking change + would mess with month-end close / freeze windows.

Dina Alvarez: Got it. Is there a recommended workaround we can give them that’s “official”? They want their finance team to reconcile to their internal PST days.

Logan W...

title: #support: 1770083321
preview:
Zendesk: New ticket linked: SUP-28463 ("Double-charged for retries")

Talia Benson: Flagging in here — customer is reporting they were double-charged due to retries/fallback. Ticket SUP-28463. They attached a CSV with `request_id`s where they see 2x billed_tokens vs their own tokenization. Asking for (1) confirmatio...
text:
Zendesk: New ticket linked: SUP-28463 ("Double-charged for retries")

Talia Benson: Flagging in here — customer is reporting they were double-charged due to retries/fallback. Ticket SUP-28463. They attached a CSV with `request_id`s where they see 2x billed_tokens vs their own tokenization. Asking for (1) confirmation if we had a bug, (2) whether their Feb invoice needs a credit, (3) how to prevent.

Talia Benson: Customer notes it happens mostly on streaming chat completions + occasional 502s. They suspect client retry, but they’re saying they only retry on network errors and they set idempotency keys.

Cole Summers: Thanks. If they’re setting idempotency keys and still seeing 2x, that points to our server-side retry/fallback path counting twice (usage event emission + retry event emission). We had an open bug in that area.

Logan Wright: Yeah, this matches ENG-4430 / PR-27463 work (“...

title: #support: 1769084201
preview:
Ava Chen: Arcadia Health escalation: streaming responses freezing mid-generation (SSE). Client keeps waiting, no [DONE]. Anyone else seeing? SUP-1842

Alex Martinez: I can reproduce in staging under load when gzip is on for SSE. Looks like flush/backpressure issue.

Dylan Brooks: +1, gateway currently applies gzip f...
text:
Ava Chen: Arcadia Health escalation: streaming responses freezing mid-generation (SSE). Client keeps waiting, no [DONE]. Anyone else seeing? SUP-1842

Alex Martinez: I can reproduce in staging under load when gzip is on for SSE. Looks like flush/backpressure issue.

Dylan Brooks: +1, gateway currently applies gzip for text/event-stream. Might be buffering chunks. We should disable gzip for SSE and force flush per token.

incident-bot: :rotating_light: tracking in SUP-1842; channel #support

Elliot Price: We saw something similar in eu-west w/ customer proxies (buffering). If we make chunks larger + periodic heartbeat, it helps.

Alex Martinez: Ive got a patch: disable gzip for content-type text/event-stream and add a 2s keepalive comment line.

Ava Chen: Can we hotfix us-east dedicated first? Customer says 1/20 requests stall; worse at peak.

Dylan Brooks: Yep. Rolling config in gatew...

Details from customer (Northwind Analytics):
- Region: us-east-1

text:
Maya Rodriguez: Escalation from Support. Customer reporting burst tail latency spikes on Hosted API. Ref: SUP-28411.

Details from customer (Northwind Analytics):
- Region: us-east-1
- Endpoint: /v1/chat/completions
- Model: `rw-chat-34b` (customer says they’re pinned to `rw-chat-34b@2026-01-22`)
- Pattern: during bursts ("~150-220 rps" for ~2-5 min), p99 jumps from ~1.8-2.4s to 8-12s. p95 also creeps to ~4-6s. They also saw a handful of request timeouts (client-side 15s).
- Workload: mixed prompt lengths (short system prompt + variable user history; some conversations go long). Streaming enabled.

Customer provided a few request IDs + time window:
- Time window: 2026-02-09 18:10-18:18 UTC
- x-request-id examples: `req_01HNN7Q2S2...`, `req_01HNN7R8KJ...`, `req_01HNN7S1ZP...`

They’re asking if we can confirm root cause / mitigate. They suspect “cache churn” on our side.

Jordan Lee: D...

title: #support: 1768104321
preview:
Priya Singh: Hey team — customer (internal ticket #C-9921) reports a sudden spike of 429s during an app-level geo-failover test. They also see a bunch of 401s intermittently for the same requests and their bill jumped overnight. Can someone help triage? Here are the headers from a repro attempt: 

Priya Singh: 
Requ...
text:
Priya Singh: Hey team — customer (internal ticket #C-9921) reports a sudden spike of 429s during an app-level geo-failover test. They also see a bunch of 401s intermittently for the same requests and their bill jumped overnight. Can someone help triage? Here are the headers from a repro attempt: 

Priya Singh: 
Request-ID: rwd-req-5f3a2
X-Region-Hint: eu-west-1
Authorization: Bearer <short-lived-token>
Sequence: initial -> failover -> retry

Priya Singh: Quick repro steps I used: 
1) Hit EU endpoint with header `X-Region-Hint: eu-west-1` repeatedly until we trigger capacity fallback. 
2) When EU returns 503/overload, the client follows the routing policy and retries in US. 
3) Some retries return 429 (throttle), others return 401. Billing shows both attempts counted. 

Priya Singh: curl I used: 
```bash
curl -v -H "Authorization: Bearer $SHORT_TOKEN" -H "X-Region-Hint: eu-west-1" \
 -...

title: #support: 1762276509
preview:
Dev Patel: Hey folks — quick guidance request on comms.

We’ve got a Dedicated customer asking (1) what “incident type” we’re calling today’s event and (2) whether the remediation SLA applies to them / what we can commit to.

text:
Dev Patel: Hey folks — quick guidance request on comms.

We’ve got a Dedicated customer asking (1) what “incident type” we’re calling today’s event and (2) whether the remediation SLA applies to them / what we can commit to.

Symptoms on their side: burst of `429` + some `5xx` spikes for ~12 min, started after they pushed a traffic ramp. We also saw elevated queue depth + GPU saturation on the dedicated pool.

What should I tell them as the incident type, and do we cite the remediation SLA clock?

Owen Phillips: +1, customer is explicitly asking “is this a Capacity incident or a Routing incident?” and wants something they can paste into their internal report.

Rachel Kim: For external comms: don’t use the internal taxonomy labels verbatim (“Capacity/Fleet Shortage”) unless they specifically ask for it. Translate it to plain language: “capacity saturation on your dedicated cluster lead...

text:
Dev Patel: pulling together a quick FAQ for common offline bundle verification errors (Support/SE use). pls reply with: (a) what the customer sees (b) likely cause (c) what we tell them to run / collect. we’ll fold into docs + day-2 runbook.

Owen Phillips: top one from pilots: `HASH_MISMATCH`.
Customer sees:
```
ERROR: Bundle verification failed (HASH_MISMATCH)
- artifact: charts/redwood-private-1.8.3.tgz
- expected_sha256: 9f3c…
- actual_sha256: 1a0d…
Install blocked.
```
Likely cause: bundle corrupted during transfer/extraction (unzip tool, CRLF conversion, “smart” AV scanning, partial copy).
What to tell them:
1) verify the bundle tar/zip checksum *before* and *after* transfer
2) re-transfer using a binary-safe method
3) avoid re-zipping; keep original bundle format
What to ask for:
- output of `installer bundle inspect <path> --verify --json`
- their file copy method + hashes on ...

text:
Rachel Kim: pulling together a support-facing runbook draft for the Private VPC Terraform modules alpha pilots. can folks drop the *common pilot issues* they’ve seen + what sections we should add? ideally link tickets/PRs so we can keep it actionable.

Colin O'Donnell: top issue so far: installer can’t pull images in private subnets bc missing ECR endpoints + private DNS not enabled. Jira: SUP-28411 (installer fails pulling images / missing ecr endpoints). workaround was “add `com.amazonaws.<region>.ecr.api` and `ecr.dkr` endpoints + enable private DNS + ensure nodes use VPC resolver”.

Colin O'Donnell: relevant installer preflight PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-private-installer/pull/19110 (detect endpoints + warn on missing ECR). once that’s in, support should ask customers to run preflight output before we chase kube issues.

text:
Dev Patel: Heads up — got a customer question in SUP-2841. They’re seeing a new `503` on `/v1/chat/completions` with message “overloaded” and asking if this is an outage vs something they should retry.

Dev Patel: They pasted this snippet:
```
HTTP/2 503
x-redwood-request-id: req_8f3b1d0c2b9a4a5d
content-type: application/json

{
 "error": {
 "message": "Service overloaded. Please retry.",
 "type": "overload_error",
 "code": "overload.shed",
 "request_id": "req_8f3b1d0c2b9a4a5d"
 }
}
```
No `Retry-After` in the example (they said sometimes they see it, sometimes not).

Owen Phillips: +1, I got a similar note from another account last week. We’re telling folks “retry with backoff” but we need canonical wording + what we promise re: headers.

Dev Patel: Here’s the draft guidance I was going to send (pls sanity check):
- 503 overload = temporary capacity protection (not a generic 5xx)
- ...

title: #support: 1741523456

text:
Sam L: Heads up — priority escalation from Acme. They see repeated 429s and duplicated invoices when using the Python client pinned to 0.8.1. Customer claims SDK retries immediately with no jitter and idempotency keys sometimes not preventing duplicates.

Maya Patel: Can you paste the minimal repro and the raw logs?

Sam L: Repro is tiny, pasted below. Running in lambda-like serverless -> bursts of concurrent cold clients.
```py
from redwood import Client
client = Client(api_key=\"REDACTED\")
resp = client.generate(prompt=\"Summarize this\") # default retry/backoff
print(resp)
```

Sam L: Logs from customer's run: 
[2025-11-03T14:12:03Z] request_id=abc123 status=429 retry_attempt=1 retry_sleep=0.0
[2025-11-03T14:12:03Z] WARN retrying request immediately (sleep=0.0)
[2025-11-03T14:12:03Z] request_id=abc124 status=429 retry_attempt=2 retry_sleep=0.0
Traceback (most recent call last):
 ....

text:
Dev Patel: Hey all — we’re getting more “can you backport this to LTS?” asks (tickets + AEs). Want to align on what *minimum info* Support needs to collect so Release Eng/Runtime can actually action it. Can we agree on an intake checklist + severity mapping?

Owen Phillips: +1. Right now it’s super variable. Some requests are “it’s broken” with no version/build ID, and then we spend 2 days chasing basics.

Connor O'Brien: Yep, we need a standard intake so we can triage in <24h. For LTS, we need enough to decide eligibility (security/Sev1/Sev2), risk, and whether it’s already fixed on main.

Talia Benson: I can turn whatever we decide here into a Support playbook section + ticket template. What fields are must-have vs nice-to-have?

Rafael Mendes: From on-call side: if this is affecting prod + customer has Dedicated/Private, we need impact + rollback options captured. Also need to know...

title: #support: 1739998122

text:
Priya Nair: hey folks — support question from an escalation. how do we quickly identify the *active optimization config version* for a customer? (they’re seeing a latency jump and want to confirm whether a new batching/caching config is live)

Priya Nair: ideally: 1) console place to look, 2) API/CLI way, and 3) any way to tie it to a specific request id

Hannah Rivera: yep, there’s a new view in Console (behind the opt-config-history flag for some roles). path: Tenant -> Optimize -> Optimization Configs -> “Active” tab.

Hannah Rivera: it shows active version per scope, so you’ll see rows like `model=llama-3.1-70b`, `region=us-east-1`, `deployment=hosted|dedicated`, and the active `config_version`.

Priya Nair: nice. do we expose it in the request logs anywhere? customers are asking “which version served this request?”

Felix Schneider: in Console -> Observability -> Requests (or Tra...

Avery Johnson: heads up, Northwind Health pinged me—on their Private env they think audit logs are missing a handful of admin events (RBAC changes) from yesterday. anyone already tracking this?

Sofia Ivanova: sounds like the HelioPay thing (SUP-10432). do we have a known issue link yet?

Connor O'Brien: can you ask them for 1-2 exact timestamps + what sink they're using (S3 vs http)? also confirm their control plane version.

Avery Johnson: will do. i'll create a support ticket if they confirm it’s more than a one-off.
